policy_name: BSR Fellowship Scheme for Science Students - UGC

sections.overview: The BSR Fellowship Scheme by the University Grants Commission (UGC) supports meritorious students pursuing Ph.D. research in science disciplines by providing financial assistance for advanced studies.
sections.objective: The scheme aims to provide opportunities to meritorious candidates to undertake advanced studies and research leading to Ph.D. degrees in sciences.
eligibility: Candidates selected for Ph.D. through regular admission procedures
University must be recognized under UGC schemes such as Centres of Excellence or Special Assistance
Candidates must register for full-time Ph.D. programmes
benefits: Up to 5 years total fellowship duration
HRA or accommodation benefits as per rules
Leave including maternity/paternity as per norms
application_process: Apply to identified universities or institutions offering Ph.D.
Selection is conducted by institution through interview
Selection committee includes experts and faculty members

faq.question: What is the objective of the scheme?
faq.answer: To support advanced research leading to Ph.D. degrees in sciences.

faq.question: Who is eligible?
faq.answer: Ph.D. candidates selected through regular admission in eligible universities.

faq.question: Is part-time Ph.D. allowed?
faq.answer: No, only full-time research candidates are eligible.

faq.question: What is the fellowship duration?
faq.answer: Up to 5 years subject to performance.

faq.question: Can fellows take other jobs?
faq.answer: No, employment or other scholarships are not allowed.
